questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A user defined function can be used multiple times in a program.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Functions are reusable blocks of code that can be called from any part of the program.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because functions are used to avoid code duplication and can be called multiple times in a program, and the reason provided is the correct explanation.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The return type of a function is specified in the function definition.
